changes.title: Drop Thursday 5:15am & 6:15am
changes.description: Remove 2 consistently underperforming slots averaging 30.6% utilization. Only 52 total attendees across entire data period.
changes.assumptions: Based on 5 class minimum for statistical confidence. Thursday 5am psychology: members mentally check out by end of week.
changes.risks: The 5-6 members who do attend these slots need to be proactively moved to alternative times.

changes.title: Add Sunday Morning Class
changes.description: Sunday 9am already hits 75% utilization — your 2nd best slot. A 10am or 10:30am class taps into proven weekend demand from your mid-morning segment.
changes.assumptions: New class ramps to 55% of existing Sunday performance. 15% of attendees are new members drawn by the added slot. $199/mo avg membership x 8 month lifespan.
changes.risks: Requires instructor availability on Sunday morning. May cannibalize some 9am attendance initially.

changes.title: Monthly Bring-a-Buddy Event
changes.description: Your Bring-a-Buddy classes average 86.3% utilization — your single highest format. Running one per month turns members into your marketing team at zero ad cost.
changes.assumptions: 50% of attendees are guests (new to studio). 46% of guests convert to paid membership (your current first-visit conversion rate). $199/mo x 8 month avg lifespan.
changes.risks: Assumes guests are genuinely new leads. If members bring existing members the revenue impact is lower but retention impact is still positive.

changes.title: Seasonal Retention Campaigns
changes.description: October and December are your two worst months at 53.1% and 51.9%. Targeted challenges and promotions in these specific months can counteract predictable seasonal drops.
changes.assumptions: A 6-week challenge pre-launched in late September and a New Year pre-commitment campaign in late November. Based on industry benchmarks of 8-12% attendance lift from structured challenges.
changes.risks: Requires marketing effort and planning. Revenue estimate is conservative — retention value is harder to quantify than acquisition.

changes.title: Optimize Instructor Scheduling
changes.description: Shaylin Collins (62.7%) and Stacy Benetti (62.4%) consistently outperform Andrea Rincon (53.9%) in fill rates. Prioritizing top instructors in prime slots lifts overall utilization.
changes.assumptions: Moving top 2 instructors to cover 30% more prime slots (Fri 5:30pm, Sun 9am, Tue/Thu evenings). Conservative 2.1% overall utilization lift based on instructor performance gap.
changes.risks: Instructor contracts and availability may limit flexibility. Member attachment to specific instructors could cause short-term resistance.

changes.title: Improve First-Visit Retention by 10%
changes.description: Currently 56.8% of first-timers never return. Reducing this by just 10% — through a structured follow-up sequence — would convert 217 more members per year.
changes.assumptions: 10% retention improvement achievable through: same-day follow-up text, 3-day post-visit email, intro offer with 48hr expiry, personal instructor follow-up call. $199/mo x 8 month avg lifespan x 46% conversion.
changes.risks: Requires systematic follow-up process and team discipline. Revenue assumes converted members stay for average lifespan — some may churn earlier.
